topic: Consolidated fl_pattern and fl_playlist MCP wrappers
context: v1.2 slice 08 adds additive domain tools beside the legacy pattern/playlist tools for parity testing and lower tool-selection overhead.
observation: fl_pattern(action, params) and fl_playlist(action, params) validate through the internal operation registry. Read-only actions use registry-built protocol payloads. Persistent writes route through safety.safe_write.
result: Pattern and playlist track reads/writes execute through the expected registry and safety paths. Prohibited pattern deletion and playlist clip editing attempts are rejected as unknown operations before bridge dispatch.
source_method: Focused FastMCP unit tests with a fake bridge, static safety audit, and registration baseline check.
known_pitfalls: fl_pattern and fl_playlist are annotated write-safe because each public function can perform persistent writes; individual action safety is enforced by registry dispatch and documented in docstrings.
Playlist scope is track metadata/control only.
Playlist clip placement, movement, deletion, and editing remain unsupported.
Pattern length writes remain documented-unconfirmed on affected FL builds.
open_questions: Run live FL Studio smoke tests when the bridge and controller are available.
next_recommended_action: Implement slice 09, the additive effect and plugin domain tools, using the same registry-first and safety-layer pattern.
